etymology.description: 沒(沒) (méi, "not") indicates the negation or absence of something. 問題(問題) (wèntí, "problem; question") combines 問(問) (wèn, "ask") and 題(題) (tí, "topic; subject") to refer to a question or problem. Together, 沒問題(沒問題) suggests the absence of a problem.
etymology.interpretation: 沒問題(沒問題) (méi wèntí, "no problem") is a reassuring expression used to indicate that something is feasible or acceptable, alleviating concerns about any obstacles.

examples.chinese: 如果你需要帮助(如果你需要幫助)，请告诉我(請告訴我)，没问题(沒問題)！
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ xūyào bāngzhù, qǐng gàosù wǒ, méi wèntí!
examples.english: If you need help, please tell me, no problem!

examples.chinese: 老板要求加班(老闆要求加班)，我说没问题(我說沒問題)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎobǎn yāoqiú jiābān, wǒ shuō méi wèntí.
examples.english: The boss asked for overtime, I said no problem.

examples.chinese: 你能修理这台电脑吗(你能修理這台電腦嗎)？没问题(沒問題)！
examples.pinyin: Nǐ néng xiūlǐ zhè tái diànnǎo ma? Méi wèntí!
examples.english: Can you fix this computer? No problem!
